places.name: Izakaya Toyo
places.description: Blowtorches, tuna, and a jolly, chain-smoking proprietor mark this beloved street-food stand.

places.name: Fudo Myo-o Statue
places.description: A moss-covered statue of a Japanese Buddhist deity stands at a quiet temple in downtown Osaka.

places.name: Gate Tower Building
places.description: The Hanshin Expressway goes right through its 5th, 6th, and 7th floors.

places.name: Tavern Pachimon Wars
places.description: This bar is filled with Star Wars merchandise from around the world, all of it unlicensed knockoffs.

places.name: Taiyoshi Hyakuban
places.description: This brothel-turned-restaurant features plenty of surprises and elaborate, extravagant architecture.

places.name: 10-Yen Vending Machines
places.description: Perhaps the world's cheapest vending machines.

places.name: Gakutensoku
places.description: Japan's first automaton went missing, only to be revived by the Osaka Science Museum.
